market_summary: S&P 500 declined this week: -0.24% Monday (April 20, close 7,109.14) and -0.63% Tuesday (April 21, close 7,064.01), snapping the Nasdaq's record 13-day winning streak. Declines driven by US-Iran ceasefire uncertainty ahead of the April 22 expiry; Trump extended the ceasefire citing Iran's 'seriously fractured government.' Strait of Hormuz remains effectively closed — Iran re-closed April 18-19, and the Iranian parliament is drafting a toll and blocking law for hostile vessels. Brent crude peaked at ~$120/barrel (+55% since war began). IEA: 'largest supply disruption in oil market history.' March CPI confirmed +3.3% YoY / +0.9% MoM (2-year high), driven by gasoline +18.9% YoY to $4.12/gallon; April CPI due May 12 and expected to approach 4.0% YoY. Fed holds at 3.50-3.75%; April 28-29 FOMC overwhelmingly expected to hold. IT sector at 31.9% of S&P 500, recovering with sector rotation out of Energy (YTD +21%) and into Technology accelerating. NVDA Vera Rubin in full production (3.3x speed improvement over Blackwell Ultra), $1T+ combined backlog reaffirmed. AMZN Q1 earnings April 29: BofA raised to $298, KeyBanc to $325.
macro_themes: 1. IRAN/HORMUZ: Ceasefire extended by Trump today (April 22) but structurally fragile — Iranian parliament drafting toll/blocking bill, Hormuz effectively still closed, IEA 'largest supply disruption in history'. Gold and capital preservation remain bid. 2. INFLATION: March CPI +3.3% YoY peak; April expected to approach 4.0% YoY due to continued energy pass-through ($4.12/gallon gasoline). Core CPI contained at 2.6%. Fed pinned at 3.50-3.75% — JPM sees no cuts 2026, possible 25bps hike Q3 2027. 3. AI CAPEX SUPERCYCLE: NVDA Vera Rubin confirmed in full production (3.3x speed over Blackwell Ultra), $1T+ combined Blackwell and Vera Rubin backlog reaffirmed. New risk: 500K-unit Rubin GPU shortfall due to HBM supply constraints — near-term headwind, not thesis invalidation. Hyperscalers $600B+ AI capex 2026. AVGO AI revenue +106% to $8.4B; custom silicon partnerships with Meta through 2029 and Alphabet through 2031. 4. GOLD: GLD WATCH — softened from $442.09 to $429.57 (-2.83%), narrowing buffer above $420 trim trigger to only 2.3% (down from 5.3%). Institutional targets intact: Goldman $5,400, JPM $6,000-$6,300, Wells Fargo $6,100-$6,300. Central banks 850 tonnes forecast 2026. Macro pillars intact but price action requires daily monitoring. 5. SECTOR ROTATION: Energy momentum fading after Hormuz-driven surge; capital rotating into Technology and Cyclicals. IT is the primary beneficiary. Q1 2026 earnings validate pro-cyclical tilt favoring IT and Financials.

Market is pre-pricing the multiple de-rating with no bounce. Full exit of 0.1739 shares at market — immediately.

STRONGER thesis flag; 0% WHT IE-domiciled accumulating ETF is the most optimal capital vehicle available. Currently 34.10% of holdings NAV vs 36% target — gap of ~$76-83. Deploy $150-170 from ASML exit proceeds immediately post-exit. S&P 500 at 7,064, IT sector at 31.9% of index, and tech-led sector rotation supports adding here without hesitation.

All formal invalidation conditions remain untriggered. Hormuz effectively still closed; Iranian parliament drafting toll and blocking law; Trump extended ceasefire citing Iran's fractured government. However, GLD softened from $442.09 to $429.57 (-2.83%) — only 2.3% above the $420 invalidation/trim trigger. Hold unconditionally; set $420 daily close alert for 25% trim trigger.

Schedule: (1) $150-170 into CSPX immediately post-ASML exit; (2) $100 reserved for MA April 30 conditional add; (3) $50 minimum buffer retained permanently; (4) Conditional FOMC action April 28-29 funded from U03A sale, not cash pool; (5) Conditional AMZN add $50-75 on strong April 29 beat from remaining cash; (6) May 3 hard deadline — all remaining deployable cash unconditionally into CSPX. new_opportunities.rationale: Broadcom confirmed as dominant custom AI silicon partner — Meta through 2029, Alphabet through 2031. AI semiconductor revenue +106% to $8.4B (43% of sales). CEO guiding >$100B AI chip revenue 2027. $73B AI backlog. EPS growth +66% FY2026, +57% FY2027. Average analyst target $466.23. Zero meaningful WHT. Gated: NAV >$5,000 AND CSPX at 36% AND ASML fully exited. Current NAV $4,258 is $742 below threshold. Wait for gate AND ideally 10-15% pullback from ~$393.

Micron is the leading US HBM producer — every NVIDIA Blackwell and Vera Rubin GPU requires HBM3e, making MU a direct pick-and-shovel play on the NVDA thesis. The 500K Rubin GPU HBM supply constraint identified this week underscores HBM scarcity, which flows through Micron's revenue and pricing. Negligible dividend = near-0% WHT. Gated: NAV >$5,000 AND 10-15% pullback from current elevated levels.

action_items: 1. CRITICAL (April 22-23, TODAY): EXIT ASML full position — sell 0.1739 shares at market (~$253.71 gross). Recommended for 3 consecutive reports. DETERIORATING: China mix 36%->19%, MATCH Act advancing (JPM: -10% EPS), stock -6% on Q1 beat. Do not wait.

2. URGENT (immediately post-ASML exit): Deploy $150-170 into CSPX to close 36% NAV target gap. Single transaction. ASML proceeds fund directly. No further deferral — May 3 hard deadline for all remaining deployable cash.

3. WATCH DAILY (GLD $420 alert): GLD at $429.57 is only 2.3% above the $420 trim trigger (down from 5.3% buffer last week). Any daily close at or below $420 = immediately sell 25% of GLD position into CSPX. Iran ceasefire extended but structurally fragile.
4. IMPORTANT (April 28-29, FOMC): Dovish signal or rate cut guidance = sell $100 of U03A, buy CSPX immediately. Hawkish or hold-no-signal = hold U03A in full.
5. IMPORTANT (April 29, 5:30 PM ET, AMZN Q1 earnings): AWS >25% AND EBIT >$20B AND raised guidance = review $50-75 AMZN add from remaining cash. Miss or AWS below 20% = HOLD/reassess.
6. IMPORTANT (April 30, MA Q1 earnings): Cross-border volume >14% AND VAS growth >20% AND guidance maintained/raised = ADD $100. In-line = HOLD. Miss or guidance cut = REDUCE/reassess.
7. MAY 3 HARD DEADLINE: All remaining deployable cash unconditionally into CSPX. Retain $50 minimum buffer. No extensions.
8. WATCH (May 20, NVDA Q1 FY2027 earnings): Monitor vs $78.8B revenue consensus, Vera Rubin ramp update, HBM supply constraint resolution. Pre-earnings add only on $180-185 pullback.
